title: Fixing the LinkedList Cycle Detection Function

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be in the form: n and then head of linked list (represented by node values).
statement.outputFormat: Return true if linked list has a cycle, false otherwise.
statement.notes: The linked list is represented by node values.

examples.explanation: No cycle in the linked list.
Cycle in the linked list (e.g. node 2 points to node 1)
No cycle in the linked list.

hints.text: Use Floyd's cycle-finding algorithm (tortoise and hare) to detect the cycle.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Floyd's cycle-finding algorithm is a clever way to detect cycles in linked lists.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution uses Floyd's cycle-finding algorithm with two pointers, the slow and the fast pointer.
commonMistakes: Incorrect implementation of Floyd's cycle-finding algorithm

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool hasCycle(ListNode* head) { if (head == nullptr || head->next == nullptr) return false; bool hasCycle = false; ListNode* slow = head; ListNode* fast = head; while (slow != nullptr && fast != nullptr && fast->next != nullptr) { if (slow == fast) { hasCycle = true; break; } slow = slow->next; fast = fast->next->next; } return hasCycle; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public boolean hasCycle(ListNode head) { if (head == null || head.next == null) return false; boolean hasCycle = false; ListNode slow = head; ListNode fast = head; while (slow != null && fast != null && fast.next != null) { if (slow == fast) { hasCycle = true; break; } slow = slow.next; fast = fast.next.next; } return hasCycle; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def hasCycle(self, head: ListNode) -> bool: if head is None or head.next is None: return False hasCycle = False slow = head fast = head while slow is not None and fast is not None and fast.next is not None: if slow == fast: hasCycle = True break slow = slow.next fast = fast.next.next return hasCycle
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function hasCycle(head) { if (!head || !head.next) return false; let hasCycle = false; let slow = head; let fast = head; while (slow && fast && fast.next) { if (slow === fast) { hasCycle = true; break; } slow = slow.next; fast = fast.next.next; } return hasCycle; }

solutions.python: import sys; class ListNode: def __init__(self, x): self.val = x; self.next = None; def hasCycle(self, head: ListNode) -> bool: if head is None or head.next is None: return False hasCycle = False slow = head fast = head while slow is not None and fast is not None and fast.next is not None: if slow == fast: hasCycle = True break slow = slow.next fast = fast.next.next return hasCycle; t = ListNode(1); t.next = ListNode(2); t.next.next = t; print(hasCycle(t));
solutions.cpp: #include <iostream> using namespace std; struct ListNode { int val; ListNode* next; ListNode(int x) : val(x), next(NULL) {} }; bool hasCycle(ListNode* head) { if (head == nullptr || head->next == nullptr) return false; bool hasCycle = false; ListNode* slow = head; ListNode* fast = head; while (slow != nullptr && fast != nullptr && fast->next != nullptr) { if (slow == fast) { hasCycle = true; break; } slow = slow->next; fast = fast->next->next; } return hasCycle; } int main() { ListNode t(1); t.next = new ListNode(2); t.next->next = t; cout << boolalpha << hasCycle(&t) << endl; return 0; }
